Umuganda-T

Tracking community action in Rwanda

Home About Feedback Login Contact

Community, accountability, action

Umuganda-T keeps Rwanda's obligation work visible and organized.

See attendance, follow community engagements, manage feedback, and support participation for adults taking part in Umuganda.

Get Started Learn More

About the App

Designed for stronger community organization.

Umuganda-T is built to show how work is scheduled, track who attended, flag absences, and help communities keep transparent records.

Community Planning

Present upcoming Umuganda events clearly so citizens know when and where to join.

Attendance Tracking

Capture who came, who missed, and highlight members over 18 for any follow-up actions.

Feedback Approval

Submit improvement ideas and get quick status feedback on approval or next steps.

Learn More

Deep dive into Umuganda-T features and benefits.

Explore advanced tools for community management, data insights, and integration with local government systems.

Data Analytics

Get detailed reports on participation trends, demographic breakdowns, and historical attendance data.

Integration with Gov Systems

Seamlessly connect with Rwanda's local administration for verified citizen data and compliance tracking.

Mobile Accessibility

Access the app on any device with responsive design optimized for smartphones and tablets.

Real-time Notifications

Receive instant alerts for event updates, attendance confirmations, and community announcements.

Feedback

Send your update or idea for Umuganda-T.

Share your thoughts and receive a simulated approval response from the community coordinator.

Access

Login or register for your Umuganda-T account.

Choose email or phone login, or create an account if you are new.

Login

Don't have an account? Register here.

Register

New user and leader accounts must be approved by the admin before they can log in.

Already have an account? Login here.

Dashboard

Welcome back to Umuganda-T.

Use the sidebar to navigate notifications, feedback, attendance, and settings.

    Notifications

    • Reminder: Umuganda starts this Saturday at 8:00 AM.
    • Attendance report for March has been approved.
    • New community cleanup event created for next month.

    Upcoming Events

    Find where your next community work will take place:

    • Tree Planting - Nyarugenge

      Location: Sector Office Grounds | Sat, April 27, 8:00 AM

    • Drainage Cleaning - Gasabo

      Location: Kibagabaga Main Road | Sat, May 25, 8:00 AM

    My Tasks

    Assigned work for current and upcoming Umuganda sessions:

    • Clear overgrowth from the roadside (Nyarugenge) - Pending
    • Distribute seedlings to village elders (Nyarugenge) - In Progress
    • Register new participants at the assembly point (Gasabo) - Scheduled

    Access Control

    Approve member accounts, promote users to leaders, and decide which leaders can manage tasks and event locations.

          • Road safety campaign idea - Approved.
          • Tool distribution request - In review.
          • Weekend training session - Scheduled.

          Attendance Overview

          Current participation metrics:

          • Total tracked adults: 42
          • Attendance rate this month: 85%
          • Pending follow-up cases: 6
          Download Attendance Report

          Leader Tools

          Leaders can add events, update event location, adjust tasks, and collect Umuganda contribution goals after admin approval.

          Deposit Umuganda Contribution

          Users can deposit contribution and see where the next Umuganda will take place.

          Settings

          Manage your account and notification preferences.

          Contact

          Connect with the Umuganda-T support team.

          Reach out for help, feedback, or questions about attending community work.

          Support Team

          For app support and community coordination questions, contact us using the form.

          • Email: support@umuganda-t.rw
          • Phone: +250 789 123 456
          • Office: Kigali, Rwanda
